worth_it_explanation: Yahoo ConnectID is Yahoo DSP's native identity solution, built on Yahoo's logged-in user graph rather than on third-party cookies. An account that is not actively using ConnectID for addressability, has not mapped its first-party data to ConnectID, and does not know how much of its targeted supply carries ConnectID signals is bidding with less signal than a properly configured account on CTV, mobile app, and authenticated web inventory. These checks are UI inspection: the identity and audience configuration settings, the first-party data onboarding status, and whatever identity coverage view the platform exposes. The fixes are configuration changes, not rebuilds.
intro: Yahoo ConnectID is a consent-based identity solution built on Yahoo's proprietary Identity Graph, which is derived from deterministic data across Yahoo's owned-and-operated properties. It enables addressable targeting on inventory that does not carry third-party cookies, including connected TV (CTV), mobile app, and authenticated web environments. Because Yahoo DSP is both a demand-side platform (DSP) and a supply-side platform with substantial owned inventory, ConnectID is deeply integrated into how the platform finds and prices addressable users. These checks confirm that the account is using ConnectID for addressability, that the advertiser's first-party data is connected to ConnectID so audiences and suppression lists carry across environments, and that the team understands how much of its targeted supply actually carries ConnectID signals.

audit_checks.title: ConnectID is not used for addressability, leaving the account dependent on third-party cookies on inventory where they are absent
audit_checks.impact_rationale: An account that does not use ConnectID for targeting cannot reach authenticated audiences on CTV, mobile app, or the growing share of open web inventory where third-party cookies are absent. CTV in particular has never supported third-party cookies, and Yahoo's CTV supply has expanded significantly. Addressable reach on that inventory requires ConnectID or a comparable signal; without it, the account bids on CTV as effectively anonymous inventory.

educate.base:
Yahoo ConnectID is built on Yahoo's Identity Graph, which is populated by deterministic, consented data from users logged into Yahoo's services: Yahoo Mail, Yahoo Finance, Yahoo News, and related properties. When a user visits a publisher site that has implemented ConnectID, Yahoo resolves that visit to an entry in the Identity Graph. A buyer in Yahoo DSP can then target or suppress that user across Yahoo-owned inventory and across publisher supply that has implemented ConnectID, regardless of whether a third-party cookie is present.

On CTV and mobile app environments, third-party cookies have never existed. ConnectID is the primary way Yahoo DSP reaches authenticated audiences in those environments. On open web inventory, ConnectID allows targeting on publishers that require authentication, such as news sites with registration walls, where cookie-based identifiers are becoming less reliable as browsers restrict third-party cookie access.

An account that is not using ConnectID for addressability falls back to contextual signals or probabilistic identifiers on those environments. First-party audience segments built from the Yahoo Dot pixel or uploaded customer data cannot match against authenticated users where ConnectID is the only available identifier. The result is that the account loses precision on an increasing share of the supply it is buying.

A few signs point to ConnectID not being used for addressability:
  • CTV and mobile app lines show very low or zero audience match rates against first-party segments
  • The account's identity or audience settings show no active ConnectID configuration
  • Lines running on CTV inventory report delivery but show no audience matching against named segments
You may want to confirm the identity configuration at the advertiser level in Yahoo DSP settings before reviewing individual line audience match rates. An enablement gap and a mapping gap can look similar in line-level reporting but have different fixes.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: Retail advertisers that capture email addresses at checkout have the type of first-party data ConnectID is designed to use. If ConnectID is not active, the account cannot match those known customers against authenticated impressions on CTV or mobile app. Retargeting and suppression against purchasers works only on the share of open web inventory where a third-party cookie is present, which shrinks as browser policies tighten.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: Lead-generation advertisers that collect email at form submission can use ConnectID to suppress known leads from top-of-funnel lines and retarget warm prospects on CTV. Without ConnectID active, suppression lists built from submitted leads cannot match against authenticated impressions, and the account may continue bidding on users it has already converted.

educate.learn_more_note: Authoritative documentation on enabling ConnectID for addressability at the advertiser level, including identity settings and participation requirements, is available in the Yahoo DSP Help Center, which requires a Yahoo DSP login to access.

impactful_updates.update_title: ConnectID CTV scale expands across major streaming platforms (November 2025)
impactful_updates.update_description: Yahoo ConnectID expanded across a broad set of programmatic CTV supply, including AMC Networks, DIRECTV Advertising, LG Ads Solutions, Paramount, Tubi, NBCUniversal, and FreeWheel. Advertisers using ConnectID can now tie outcomes such as a mobile purchase to CTV ad exposure across a much larger portion of the CTV supply.

investigate.intro: Confirm whether ConnectID is active in the advertiser's identity configuration and whether CTV or mobile app lines show any audience match against first-party segments.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: Navigate to the advertiser's account settings in Yahoo DSP and locate the identity or data configuration section. Confirm whether ConnectID is listed as an active identifier type for the account.
Open a CTV or mobile app line under one of the advertiser's active campaigns. Review the audience targeting configuration and note whether any first-party segments are applied.

If first-party segments are applied on the CTV or mobile app line, check whether those segments show a non-zero estimated reach or match rate on that environment. A match rate of zero on a segment that shows healthy reach on desktop web suggests that ConnectID is not bridging the audience to authenticated CTV impressions.

Review any identity or addressability summary available at the account or campaign level. Yahoo DSP surfaces identity testing and coverage views in some account configurations; the availability of this data depends on account setup and may require contacting the Yahoo DSP account team.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether ConnectID is active in the advertiser's identity settings, and whether CTV or mobile app lines show non-zero audience match rates against first-party segments. An account with ConnectID active and measurable audience matching on authenticated inventory types passes. An account where ConnectID is off, or where it appears on but CTV lines show zero match against first-party segments, is a flag recorded with the affected line types and the identity configuration observed.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the ConnectID enablement status and the audience match rate observation on CTV or mobile app lines above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP account is not using ConnectID for addressability, leaving the account dependent on third-party cookies on CTV, mobile app, and authenticated web inventory where those cookies are absent or unavailable. First-party audience segments cannot match against authenticated users in those environments under this configuration. {{voice_recommend}} enabling ConnectID at the advertiser level, confirming any participation requirements with the Yahoo DSP account team, and verifying that CTV and mobile app lines show non-zero audience match rates after enabling.

audit_checks.title: First-party data is not mapped to ConnectID, so audience segments and suppression lists do not carry across cookie-free environments
audit_checks.impact_rationale: First-party data that exists only as a cookie-based segment or an unresolved uploaded list cannot reach authenticated users on CTV, mobile app, or browsers where third-party cookies are restricted. Retargeting and suppression lists built on that data underdeliver on a growing share of the supply the account is buying, and the audience match rates in reporting do not surface the shortfall clearly.

educate.base:
Activating ConnectID at the account level is a precondition for identity-based targeting, but it does not automatically make existing first-party segments ConnectID-addressable. The advertiser's customer data, whether site visitors captured by the Yahoo Dot pixel or customer lists uploaded to Yahoo DSP, has to be explicitly connected to ConnectID through the data onboarding process before those segments can match against authenticated bid requests.

For uploaded customer lists, Yahoo DSP accepts hashed email addresses and phone numbers, which the platform maps to ConnectID entries in Yahoo's Identity Graph. A list that was uploaded without going through this mapping step contains raw or unresolved identifiers that cannot match on CTV or mobile app inventory. Yahoo DSP also supports integrations with data platforms and CDPs (Customer Data Platforms) that can send audiences directly to the advertiser's Yahoo DSP seat; those integrations must be configured to deliver the identifier type the platform can map to ConnectID.

A segment built before a proper ConnectID mapping workflow was in place, or populated continuously from a source that has not been connected to the mapping workflow, behaves as a cookie-only segment on environments where ConnectID is the only available identifier. The segment appears to exist in the platform but matches zero users on CTV and mobile app.

A few signs point to the mapping gap:
  • First-party segments show healthy estimated reach on desktop web but near-zero reach estimates on CTV or mobile app
  • Suppression lists built from known customers do not reduce delivery to those users on CTV campaigns
  • The data onboarding configuration does not show hashed email or phone as the identifier type, or shows the identifier type without a ConnectID resolution step
You may want to compare the reach estimate for a high-value first-party segment across environment types. A segment with strong desktop reach and negligible CTV or mobile app reach is a reliable indicator that the underlying identifiers have not been mapped to ConnectID.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: Purchase data and cart-abandonment segments are among the highest-value first-party lists for a retail advertiser. If those lists have not been mapped to ConnectID, retargeting and suppression against known purchasers works only on the share of open web inventory where a third-party cookie is present. CTV retargeting and purchase suppression require ConnectID-mapped data to function on that environment.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: A lead suppression list that prevents known leads from seeing acquisition ads depends on the suppression data matching the same identifier that appears in the bid request. Without ConnectID mapping, suppression works on cookie-based inventory but not on CTV or mobile app, where leads who converted on desktop may continue to receive top-of-funnel ads.

educate.learn_more_note: Authoritative documentation on mapping first-party data to ConnectID, including supported identifier types and the data onboarding workflow in Yahoo DSP, is available in the Yahoo DSP Help Center, which requires a Yahoo DSP login to access. The Prebid.org documentation linked above describes the publisher-side ConnectID implementation, which provides context for how hashed email addresses are used to resolve ConnectID entries.

investigate.intro: Confirm whether the advertiser's first-party segments have been mapped to ConnectID, and compare segment reach estimates across environment types to surface any cookie-only segments.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: In Yahoo DSP, navigate to the advertiser and open the Audiences section. For each first-party segment used in active campaigns, note the identifier type listed for the segment source: whether it is based on the Yahoo Dot pixel, an uploaded list, or a data partner integration.

Select a high-value retargeting or suppression segment and review its reach or size breakdown by environment type if that breakdown is available. A segment with substantial desktop reach but near-zero CTV or mobile app reach suggests the underlying identifiers are cookie-based and have not been mapped to ConnectID.

Review the data onboarding or audience upload configuration. Confirm whether uploaded customer lists use hashed email or phone as the identifier type, and whether the platform shows a ConnectID resolution step in the data pipeline.

Check any active data partner or CDP (Customer Data Platform) integrations. Confirm whether those integrations are delivering audience data with an identifier type that Yahoo DSP can map to ConnectID, or whether they are delivering a cookie-based identifier only.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per high-value first-party segment, whether ConnectID mapping is confirmed in the data configuration and whether the segment shows non-trivial reach on CTV or mobile app. A segment with confirmed ConnectID mapping and measurable cross-environment reach passes. A segment where the data configuration shows no ConnectID resolution step, or where the reach estimate on CTV or mobile app is near zero while desktop reach is healthy, is a flag recorded with the segment name and the environments where reach is absent.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the ConnectID mapping status and the cross-environment reach observation for each first-party segment reviewed above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that first-party audience segments on {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP account have not been mapped to ConnectID. The segments contain identifiers that do not resolve against authenticated users on CTV, mobile app, or open web inventory where third-party cookies are absent, so retargeting and suppression logic underdelivers on a share of the targeted supply. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the data onboarding configuration for high-value first-party segments, confirming that uploaded lists use hashed email or phone as the identifier type with a ConnectID resolution step, and verifying that segments show non-trivial reach on CTV and mobile app before relaunching affected retargeting or suppression lines.

audit_checks.title: ConnectID coverage across targeted supply is not understood, so the account does not know how much of its spend is addressable through ConnectID
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Without visibility into what share of targeted impressions carry ConnectID signals, the account cannot tell whether its identity configuration is working across the supply it is actually buying. A campaign that appears correctly configured for ConnectID may still serve a significant portion of its impressions on supply where ConnectID has no effect, and standard performance metrics do not surface this gap.

educate.base:
Not all supply flowing through Yahoo DSP carries ConnectID signals in bid requests. ConnectID adoption varies by publisher, supply source, and environment type. Yahoo-owned inventory, authenticated publisher integrations, and CTV supply that has implemented ConnectID have higher coverage; open exchange display inventory from publishers without authentication flows has lower coverage. An account running campaigns across a mix of these environments may find that a portion of its impressions are served on supply where ConnectID has no effect, even when the account is fully configured.

Yahoo DSP has made ConnectID coverage broadly available across its own supply: as of late 2025, the platform reported that 90% of Yahoo DSP spend runs on ConnectID-enabled supply, and CTV integrations now include a substantial set of major streaming publishers. However, the mix of supply a specific account buys depends on targeting parameters, inventory settings, and deal configurations, and the account-level picture may differ from the platform-level figure.

Understanding ConnectID coverage for the account's actual supply mix helps the team confirm that the identity configuration is producing the reach and measurement quality that the setup implies. It also provides a basis for deciding whether to shift budget toward higher-coverage environments, such as direct CTV deals or premium authenticated publishers, when addressable reach is the campaign goal.

A few signs point to the coverage picture being unclear:
  • The account has not reviewed identity match or ConnectID signal coverage broken down by supply source or environment type
  • Campaign reporting does not include a dimension for identity or addressability
  • The team assumes ConnectID is working because it is enabled, without having confirmed coverage on the supply the campaigns are actually buying
You may want to request an identity coverage or addressability view from the Yahoo DSP account team if that data is not visible in the standard reporting interface, and to review coverage at least once per major campaign flight.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: Retail campaigns that target known shoppers across CTV and open web benefit most from understanding the ConnectID coverage split across the supply mix. If the retargeting segment is mapped to ConnectID but the campaign is buying predominantly open web display from publishers without authentication, the ConnectID configuration has limited effect on most of the impressions served. Shifting budget toward CTV or authenticated publishers may improve addressable reach more than further refinement of the audience segment.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: Lead-generation campaigns that rely on suppression lists to manage frequency against converted leads need ConnectID-passing supply for that suppression to function. Knowing what share of the campaign's supply carries ConnectID signals tells the team how much of the frequency and suppression logic is actually in effect across the delivery.

educate.learn_more_note: Authoritative documentation on identity coverage reporting, addressability views, and how to interpret ConnectID signal data within Yahoo DSP is available in the Yahoo DSP Help Center, which requires a Yahoo DSP login to access. For accounts with an active Yahoo DSP account team relationship, coverage data may also be available through managed reporting.

investigate.intro: Review the identity or addressability reporting available in the account to understand what share of targeted supply carries ConnectID signals, broken down by environment and supply source where possible.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: Open the reporting section in Yahoo DSP and look for a reporting dimension related to identity, addressability, or ConnectID signal coverage. The availability of this breakdown depends on account configuration and the reporting views enabled for the account.

If an identity or addressability breakdown is available, note the share of impressions that carried a ConnectID signal versus those that did not, broken down by environment type: CTV, mobile app, and desktop web.

Review the supply source breakdown for lines running on open web or open exchange inventory. Yahoo-owned inventory and authenticated publisher supply generally carry higher ConnectID coverage than open exchange display from unauthenticated publishers.

If coverage data is not visible in the standard reporting interface, note this in your audit findings and consider requesting the data through the Yahoo DSP account team. The account team may be able to provide a ConnectID coverage or addressability view for the account's recent spend.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether ConnectID coverage data is available in reporting, and if so, what share of targeted impressions carried a ConnectID signal across CTV, mobile app, and desktop web. An account where coverage data is available and shows non-trivial ConnectID reach on the supply types the campaigns target passes. An account where coverage data is unavailable, or where ConnectID signal coverage is near zero across all environment types despite ConnectID being enabled, is a flag recorded with the affected campaigns and the environments where coverage is absent or unknown.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the ConnectID signal coverage breakdown by environment type, or note that the breakdown is unavailable in current reporting, above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP account does not have a clear picture of ConnectID coverage across its targeted supply. The account may be configured for ConnectID but still serving a portion of its impressions on supply that carries no ConnectID signal, which means the identity configuration has limited effect on those impressions. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the identity or addressability reporting available in the platform to understand what share of targeted supply carries ConnectID signals by environment type, and considering whether budget reallocation toward CTV or authenticated publisher supply would improve addressable reach across the campaign mix.
